author: mariusvanderwijden
content: I misunderstood the question here. I agree that the EL doesn't need the payloads from the cl. We will trigger a full sync in this case

author: lukaszrozmej
content: <@755590043632140352> IMO it is fine. From Nethermind perspective FCU is crucial to do a catch-up sync, NewPayload is not enough.

author: lukaszrozmej
content: and by catch-up I mean it will grab payloads from network and execute them

author: potuz
content: So no danger that we could trigger a snap sync?

author: mariusvanderwijden
content: <@402853454126776320> Once we finished a snap sync, we never go back to it no matter what, right? (i am pretty sure)

author: lukaszrozmej
content: <@755590043632140352> it would depend on implementation, but we agreed on Paris that we shouldn't do catch-up by syncing state but only by processing blocks. If someone is offline for months and wants to catch-up we recommend fresh sync

author: potuz
content: Thanks I'm starting to suspect that we can greatly simplify CL syncing without dropping the trust assumptions of today
